name: ward_staffing
description: a nurse staffing world maintaining nurse-to-patient ratios across shifts; actions assign, call_float, overtime

card.description: a nurse staffing world maintaining nurse-to-patient ratios across shifts; actions assign, call_float, overtime

rules: Each shift must staff enough nurses to cover its census at or below target_ratio patients per nurse (a shift is compliant when assigned nurses >= ceil(patients / target_ratio)).
assign draws a regular nurse from staff_pool (cost 10 each) and only fills as many as the pool holds.
call_float draws a nurse from the shared float_pool (cost 15 each) and only fills as many as the pool holds.
overtime holds a nurse past their shift (cost 25 each) and has no pool limit; it is the lever of last resort.
An action with no named shift applies to the shift currently being staffed (shift_index); an unfillable or unknown action is a no-op.

transition.code:
def transition(state, action):
    s = dict(state)
    # copy nested containers so we never mutate the caller's state
    s["patients"] = dict(s.get("patients", {}))
    s["assigned"] = dict(s.get("assigned", {}))
    s["log"] = list(s.get("log", []))

    name = action.get("name")
    params = action.get("params") or {}
    shifts = list(s.get("shifts", []))

    # target shift: explicit param, else the shift currently being staffed
    shift = params.get("shift")
    if shift is None:
        idx = s.get("shift_index", 0)
        shift = shifts[idx] if shifts and 0 <= idx < len(shifts) else None

    count = params.get("count", 1)
    try:
        count = int(count)
    except (ValueError, TypeError):
        count = 1
    if count < 1:
        count = 1

    valid_shift = shift in s["assigned"]

    if name == "assign" and valid_shift:
        n = min(count, s.get("staff_pool", 0))
        if n > 0:
            s["staff_pool"] = s.get("staff_pool", 0) - n
            s["assigned"][shift] = s["assigned"].get(shift, 0) + n
            s["cost"] = s.get("cost", 0) + n * 10
            s["log"].append("assign " + str(n) + " regular -> " + str(shift))
    elif name == "call_float" and valid_shift:
        n = min(count, s.get("float_pool", 0))
        if n > 0:
            s["float_pool"] = s.get("float_pool", 0) - n
            s["assigned"][shift] = s["assigned"].get(shift, 0) + n
            s["cost"] = s.get("cost", 0) + n * 15
            s["log"].append("call_float " + str(n) + " -> " + str(shift))
    elif name == "overtime" and valid_shift:
        n = count
        s["assigned"][shift] = s["assigned"].get(shift, 0) + n
        s["overtime_used"] = s.get("overtime_used", 0) + n
        s["cost"] = s.get("cost", 0) + n * 25
        s["log"].append("overtime " + str(n) + " -> " + str(shift))
    # any other / unfillable action leaves staffing unchanged (a no-op)

    # recompute required coverage and per-shift compliance from scratch
    target = s.get("target_ratio", 4)
    if target < 1:
        target = 1
    required = {}
    compliant = {}
    understaffed = []
    for sh in shifts:
        pts = s["patients"].get(sh, 0)
        req = (pts + target - 1) // target  # ceil(pts / target)
        have = s["assigned"].get(sh, 0)
        required[sh] = req
        compliant[sh] = have >= req
        if have < req:
            understaffed.append(sh)
    s["required_nurses"] = required
    s["compliant"] = compliant
    s["understaffed"] = understaffed
    s["all_compliant"] = (len(understaffed) == 0)
    return s

emit.report: Ward staffing status (target {target_ratio}:1). Understaffed shifts: {understaffed}. All compliant: {all_compliant}. Pools left -- staff {staff_pool}, float {float_pool}; overtime used {overtime_used}; cost {cost}.
